heading: Equipment and personnel requirements under tank vessel and facility response plans
label: 2735 Equipment and personnel requirements under tank vessel and facility response plans
notes: References in TextThe Trans-Alaska Pipeline Authorization Act, referred to in subsec. (a), is title II of , , , which is classified generally to chapter 34 (§ 1651 et seq.) of Title 43, Public Lands. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 93–15387 Stat. 584section 1651 of Title 43Nov. 16, 1973

Amendments1992—Subsec. (a). substituted “tanker loading cargo at” for “tank vessel operating on Prince William Sound, or” and directed the insertion of “and a response plan for such a facility,” after “( et seq.).”, which was executed by making the insertion after “( et seq.),” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 102–38843 U.S.C. 165143 U.S.C. 1651

Amendments1992—Subsec. (a). substituted “tanker loading cargo at” for “tank vessel operating on Prince William Sound, or” and directed the insertion of “and a response plan for such a facility,” after “( et seq.).”, which was executed by making the insertion after “( et seq.),” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 102–38843 U.S.C. 165143 U.S.C. 1651

children.text: prepositioned oil spill containment and removal equipment in communities and other strategic locations within the geographic boundaries of Prince William Sound, including escort vessels with skimming capability; barges to receive recovered oil; heavy duty sea boom, pumping, transferring, and lightering equipment; and other appropriate removal equipment for the protection of the environment, including fish hatcheries;

the establishment of an oil spill removal organization at appropriate locations in Prince William Sound, consisting of trained personnel in sufficient numbers to immediately remove, to the maximum extent practicable, a worst case discharge or a discharge of 200,000 barrels of oil, whichever is greater;
training in oil removal techniques for local residents and individuals engaged in the cultivation or production of fish or fish products in Prince William Sound;
practice exercises not less than 2 times per year which test the capacity of the equipment and personnel required under this paragraph; and
periodic testing and certification of equipment required under this paragraph, as required by the Secretary.
the term “Prince William Sound” means all State and Federal waters within Prince William Sound, Alaska, including the approach to Hinchenbrook Entrance out to and encompassing Seal Rocks; and

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a vessel, a discharge in adverse weather conditions of its entire cargo; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a facility, the largest foreseeable discharge in adverse weather conditions.
